qualifying_org_definition: Governmental or nonprofit organization (e.g., food pantry, homeless shelter); for-profit volunteer work not allowable. HAR §17-684.1-58 defines 'Working' to include 'unpaid work, verified under standards established by the department,' which encompasses nonprofit volunteer activity as a standalone path distinct from the formal E&T work program.
hours_formula_detail: HAR §17-684.1-58 explicitly states 'twenty hours a week averaged monthly means eighty hours a month' — direct adoption of 7 CFR §273.24 federal baseline.
accepts_outofstate_detail: Hawaii DHS FAQ and HAR §17-684.1-58 describe qualifying orgs as 'governmental or nonprofit organization (e.g., food pantry, homeless shelter)' with no geographic restriction stated. No source addresses in-state vs. out-of-state distinction. Federal default (any qualifying nonprofit, 7 CFR §273.24) should apply absent a state restriction.
geographic_waivers: Hawaii has special noncontiguous-state status under OBBBA (P.L. 119-21): waivers available through 2028 if state unemployment rate is ≥1.5x the national average, rather than the standard 10% threshold that applies to other states. No county-level approved waivers were identified in public FNA listings as of June 2026, but the special eligibility criteria means partial waiver coverage remains possible depending on unemployment rates. State-level enforcement has been 'partial' since November 1, 2025.
submission_deadline: Changes in work hours must be reported within 10 days of the change (HAR §17-684.1-61). Some recipients submit a Monthly Eligibility Report Form (MERF); specific deadline for work-hour verification letters not stated in public sources.
notes:
Hawaii enforces ABAWD requirements as of November 1, 2025 (expanded age range 18-64); the state has a special carve-out under the One Big Beautiful Bill Act (P.L. 119-21, July 2025) maintaining waiver eligibility through 2028 for areas meeting unemployment thresholds, making full enforcement status 'partial.' HAR §17-684.1-58 (Subchapter 5) defines 'Working' to include 'unpaid work, verified under standards established by the department,' which is a clean standalone lane — no EDP, MOU, or E&T enrollment required before volunteer hours count; E&T is described by DHS as 'voluntary.' No state-specific verification form name was found in public sources; documentation appears to be a caseworker-verified self-report (employer letter or written statement of hours).

REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-21): Conducted four targeted adversarial searches plus direct page fetches of the HI DHS FAQ, E&T program page, SNAP Rights & Responsibilities page, LII regulation text (§§17-684.1-59 and -65), Governor's Office news release, and a somedoing.com national volunteer-verification survey. Searched angles: (a) Hawaii SNAP E&T State Plan FY2026/FY27 volunteer/community service track; (b) Hawaii SNAP work registration / ABAWD case manager / employment plan; (c) Hawaii SNAP ABAWD approved volunteer site list / nonprofit verification form site:.gov; (d) Hawaii food stamps work requirement legal aid nonprofit verification 2025-2026. Key adversarial findings and why Tier 1 stands: (1) E&T is explicitly voluntary — multiple DHS-sourced texts state 'SNAP E&T is a voluntary program'; the eligibility worker E&T screening is a routine intake offer, not a gate. (2) No approved-site list found — DHS FAQs describe qualifying orgs generically ('governmental or nonprofit organization, e.g., food pantry or homeless shelter') with no state registry or pre-clearance requirement. (3) No mandatory prestep found — no EDP, employment plan, or formal assignment requirement precedes standalone volunteer-hour compliance; general work registration at SNAP application (HAR §17-684.1 Subchapter 1) is a separate SNAP-wide obligation that does not gate ABAWD compliance via the volunteer lane. (4) HAR §17-684.1-58 'Working' definition confirmed to include unpaid/volunteer work 'verified under standards established by the department,' with no E&T enrollment gate in the regulatory text. (5) Verification instrument remains unnamed in all public sources; operational practice appears to be caseworker-verified written statement / letter from the nonprofit — standard across states without a named form. The remaining unknown (no named verification form) is an operational detail that does not affect tier classification. Confidence elevated to 'high': the regulatory text (HAR §17-684.1-58) directly authorizes the standalone volunteer lane, multiple DHS-official sources confirm E&T is voluntary, and no hidden prestep was discovered across all adversarial search angles.

EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): (1) Hours formula: HAR §17-684.1-58 explicitly defines 'twenty hours a week averaged monthly means eighty hours a month' — confirmed federal_80 baseline, 80 hours/month. (2) Out-of-state nonprofit: All public sources (DHS FAQ, HAR §17-684.1-58) describe qualifying orgs as 'governmental or nonprofit organization' with zero geographic restriction stated anywhere; policy is silent on in-state vs. out-of-state — federal default (any qualifying nonprofit) applies. (3) Geographic waivers: Hawaii has special noncontiguous-state status under OBBBA through 2028, allowing waivers if unemployment ≥1.5x national average rather than 10% threshold; no specific county-level approved waivers identified in public FNA listings as of June 2026, but partial coverage possible. (4) Submission UX: Changes in work hours must be reported within 10 days (HAR §17-684.1-61); some households submit Monthly Eligibility Report Form (MERF); change reports go paper/mail/in-person to processing center — no dedicated online ABAWD hours portal found; submission_deadline is 'within 10 days of change' per §17-684.1-61. (5) Form fields: No named verification form found; applied reasonable default baseline fields for a nonprofit letter.
